subject: A Review of Set Theory
question: Use a property to give a description of each of the following sets. (a) $\{a, e, i, o, u\}$. (b) $\{1, 3, 5, 7, 9\}$.
explanation: (a) $\{x \mid x \text{ is a vowel}\}$. (b) $\{n \in \mathbb{N} \mid n \text{ is odd and less than 10}\}$ The first arithmetic operation involving sets that we consider is the equality of two sets. Two sets $A$ and $B$ are said to be equal if and only if they contain the same elements. We write $A = B$. For non-equal sets we write $A \ne B$. In this case, the two sets do not contain the same elements.
